(1 inline comment)
Patch Set 7: (1 inline comment)

Patch Set 7:

Part of ORNL contract, should be landed to lustre-2.2.
Oleg, should I rebuild the patch against the latest master before accepted?

Patch Set 7: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(7 inline comments)

My first main concern is whether adding the DEFACL buffer breaks the RPC network protocol and interoperability with older clients?

My second concern is whether it would be better to create a generic mechanism for returning xattrs to the client, instead of (or in addition to) the DEFACL, so that we could address a number of performance issues related to repeatedly fetching xattrs to the client.  Since xattrs are becoming increasingly widely used for security, samba, NFS, and other reasons (see LU-549), it makes sense to try and address these issues together.

If we have to do extra work to maintain RPC compatibility, then instead of adding a reply buffer, flags, etc. that is only going to fix the DEFACL issue, it would be better to make a generic xattr fetch mechanism in the reply (e.g. as many xattrs as will fit into the reply buffer, given that the server knows the size already), and they can be cached on the client either under the MDS_INODELOCK_UPDATE bit or at some later time under a separate MDS_INODELOCK_XATTR bit.

Patch Set 7: (1 inline comment)

I will re-implement a general packaged xattr framework to achieve:
1) both posix_acl and def_acl can be returned through single RPC reply
2) no interoperability issues between 1.8 client and 2.2 server
3) no interoperability issues between 2.1 client and 2.2 server
4) no interoperability issues between 2.2 client and 2.1 server
5) the packaged xattr can be extended to contain more other xattrs in the future without introducing interoperability issues.
Uploaded patch set 8.

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/3121/

Patch Set 8:

The patch has been verified for interoperability as following:

1) 1.8 client + 2.2 server
2) 2.1 client + 2.2 server
3) 2.2 client + 2.1 server

without new interoperability issues were introduced.

Patch Set 8: (11 inline comments)

I think the patch is a very good start, but I think we need to ensure that the wire protocol is flexible enough to handle multiple xattrs with arbitrary names.

I would really prefer to avoid adding yet another OBD_MD_PACKED_XATTR flag, since this field is almost running out, and it should be possible to just check OBD_CONNECT_PACKED_XATTR to decide what is in this buffer.

Patch Set 19: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

James, because this changes the protocol, and we haven't really done a proper design on this feature, we aren't planning to land this change for the current release.  There may be protocol changes in the final version, so it isn't recommended to use this patch on a system that cannot be upgraded completely (i.e. all clients + MDS at the same time).

parents.subject: LU-1534 osd: Fix LBUGs when destroying IGIF objects

commit.subject: LU-549 xattr: packaged xattr in single RPC reply
commit.message:
LU-549 xattr: packaged xattr in single RPC reply

Currently, MDS returns ACL together with other object's attributes
when replies getattr/lookup RPC. As for stating directory object,
client will send additional RPC for default ACL.

As an improvement, MDS will return packaged xattr to contain both
posix ACL and default ACL (for directory). For remote client, the
remote permission is also returned through packaged xattr.

The packaged xattr framework supports to return more other xattrs
by single RPC.

Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <yong.fan@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I8e43731ec0f360605dda1d3457f2821523fcef0d
